title: Safe Online Shopping
drDigitalIntro: Online shopping is convenient but has risks. Before entering your credit card, check: Is the URL correct (amazon.com, not amaz0n-deals.net)? Does the address bar show https:// with a padlock icon? Does the site look professional or rushed/suspicious? Stick to well-known retailers or sites you've used before. Use a credit card (not debit) — credit cards have better fraud protection.

playgroundTask.instructions: You want to buy shoes online. One of these sites is a scam. Which one?

items.label: amazon.com
items.preview: 🔒 https://www.amazon.com — Millions of products, trusted reviews, free Prime shipping. Credit card info protected by SSL encryption.

items.label: amaz0n-deals.net
items.preview: AMAZING DEALS!! 90% off ALL shoes TODAY ONLY!! Enter your credit card at checkout. No returns needed — guaranteed lowest prices!! Limited time: next 10 minutes!

playgroundTask.fakeExplanation: Red flags: 'amaz0n' uses a zero instead of 'o', it's a .net domain instead of .com, excessive exclamation marks, unbelievable discounts, and extreme time pressure — all classic scam tactics.

drDigitalSuccess: You spotted the fake store! Check the URL carefully, look for https://, and be very suspicious of deals that seem too good to be true.
drDigitalHint: Look very closely at the web address spelling. Is anything slightly off?
